name: lsp-rebuild
description: PostToolUse(Write|Edit) auto-rebuild for hexa-native LSP servers, implemented in hexa-lang (`_lsp_rebuild.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). When the agent edits an LSP grammar source matching `*/lsp/<lang>_lsp.hexa`, the prebuilt binary `~/.local/bin/<lang>-lsp-hexa` is now stale (the editor's next LSP session would still run the old grammar), so this hook rebuilds it in the BACKGROUND — `nohup env HEXA_MAC_BUILD_OK=1 hexa build <src> -o ~/.local/bin/<lang>-lsp-hexa` (detached `&` so PostToolUse returns immediately; output appends to ~/.sidecar/lsp-rebuild.log) — and emits a non-blocking advisory. This keeps the build artifact in lockstep with the canonical .hexa grammar source so a grammar change propagates without a manual rebuild step. Rationale for rebuild-not-source-run: the hexa LSP stdio server needs the `read_stdin_n_c` FFI which only links into a COMPILED binary; `hexa run` of the source can't serve interactive stdin, so the .hexa source is the SSOT and the prebuilt binary is its artifact. Fires on the `*/lsp/*_lsp.hexa` source pattern AND on hexa-lang's own LSP, which is NOT named `*_lsp.hexa` (`<repo>/self/lsp.hexa` monolith + `<repo>/self/lsp/*.hexa` submodules) — rebuilt via the repo's 3-stage orchestrator `tool/build_hexa_lsp.hexa` → `<repo>/bin/hexa-lsp` (project.tape @D s11: gate on a real precondition, no opt-out switch). Pairs with the per-language `*-lsp` wiring plugins (hexa-lsp · kosmos-lsp · n6-lsp · hxc-lsp). NO opt-out by design.
